L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-provided content in social posts, comments, and direct messages is stored and rendered without sanitization. An attacker can inject malicious JavaScript that executes in the browsers of other users, including administrators, potentially leading to session hijacking, account takeover, and wormable attacks.

L3.area: Social content creation and direct messaging content handlers
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the social feature where user-provided content for posts, comments, direct messages, and group conversation names is directly stored in the database without HTML sanitization. Malicious JavaScript injected through these content fields is executed when other users view the home feed, enabling wormable attacks and session hijacking.

ground_truth.reason: User-provided content in social posts, comments, direct messages, and group names is stored without sanitization, allowing attackers to inject and execute arbitrary JavaScript in other users' browsers.
